must_have_breakdown.evidence: 4+ years of Salesforce platform experience across multiple enterprise environments including sandbox management, environment refresh, and production support.

must_have_breakdown.skill: 5+ years experience in Salesforce Platform Management or Salesforce Architecture
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Resume states '4+ years of hands-on Salesforce platform experience' — falls short of the 5-year minimum, though total IT experience is 6+ years.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Expert-level proficiency with Copado for CI/CD and release orchestration
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Holds four Copado certifications (SFP and Metadata formats), managed end-to-end Copado deployments reducing deployment time by 30%, and has used Copado CLI and pipelines across two enterprise roles.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Deep understanding of Salesforce multi-tenant architecture

must_have_breakdown.evidence: Conducted environment refresh activities across Salesforce orgs and managed sandbox management explicitly cited under technical skills and experience.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Communication and ability to translate complex technical releases into business-friendly roadmaps
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Evidence of stakeholder management and cross-functional collaboration cited but no explicit mention of translating technical releases into business roadmaps or executive communication.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Experience managing Business Critical production environments
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Provided 24/7 production support and delivered critical production releases but no explicit 'Business Critical' tier designation or scale metrics mentioned.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Work with Salesforce's premier support tiers

must_have_breakdown.evidence: Salesforce CRM and administration experience cited; intern role included coding and QA, but deep development environment work is not demonstrated at a senior level.
Enforced 'release best practices and governance standards' in Copado pipelines, but no evidence of applying Salesforce development design patterns specifically.

reasoning: Borderline — hold pending clarification on Salesforce development depth and architecture exposure. This candidate is a strong Copado specialist and release engineer, not a Salesforce Platform Manager. Her most differentiating signal is genuine Copado expertise across both SFP and Metadata formats with four certifications and a measurable 30% deployment time reduction — that is rare and directly relevant. However, the role demands a Platform Manager who can architect a federated development strategy, establish a Center of Excellence, and manage governor limits and multi-tenant architecture — none of which appear in this resume. The entire development stack required (custom objects, workflows, triggers, SOQL, SOSL, Apex, LWC) is absent from her profile. She also falls short at 4+ years of Salesforce-specific experience versus the 5-year minimum. With a 0.35 must-have coverage ratio and missing the architectural and development pillars of this senior role, she is operating at a Mid-level Release Engineer scope, not a Platform Manager scope. Do not advance for this specific role; redirect to a Salesforce DevOps Engineer or Release Manager opening where she would be a strong hire.
strengths: COPADO MASTERY: Holds four Copado certifications spanning both SFP and Metadata formats, has actively managed Copado CI/CD pipelines across two enterprise roles, and delivered a verified 30% reduction in deployment time — a rare, quantified outcome that signals genuine platform depth, not credential collecting.

PRODUCTION RELEASE GOVERNANCE: Delivered 24/7 production support, managed hotfix coordination, conducted release risk assessments, and maintained multi-environment release schedules across enterprise telecom and tech clients — directly aligned to the production stability custodianship this role requires.

AGENTFORCE EXPOSURE: Explicitly executed Agentforce AI component deployments via VS Code and Copado CLI at Trimble — a cutting-edge capability listed as a nice-to-have that very few candidates at this level can demonstrate in a current production context.
gaps: MISSING SALESFORCE DEVELOPMENT STACK: The role requires hands-on proficiency with custom objects, workflows, triggers, SOQL, SOSL, Apex, Visualforce, and LWC — none of these appear anywhere in the resume. A Platform Manager must understand what developers are deploying; without this foundation, she cannot architect a federated development strategy or govern a Center of Excellence effectively.

NO ARCHITECTURE OR COE EXPERIENCE: The core mandate of this role is architecting a modern development strategy and establishing a Center of Excellence. There is zero evidence of Salesforce architecture work, multi-tenant architecture depth, governor limits management, or strategic platform design — making her fundamentally mismatched for a 'Manager' and 'Architect' level scope.

scoring_factors.reason: JD does not specify a maximum notice period, so no penalty is applied.
JD does not specify a work structure or location requirement, so no location penalty applies.
Candidate has 4+ years of Salesforce experience, which is above the half-of-minimum threshold of 2.5 years.
JD specifies 'Any' gender preference, so no penalty is applied.
JD specifies no college preference or exclusion, so no adjustment is applied.
